config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu diprioritaskan tanpa batas, itu mudah sekali dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan. Saya memang percaya masyarakat penting, tetapi masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari perlindungan kuat terhadap hak tiap orang, terutama bagi kelompok yang lemah atau minoritas. Kepentingan umum boleh dibatasi dalam keadaan tertentu, tetapi tidak boleh mengalahkan martabat dan kebebasan dasar individu secara otomatis.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to excuse government overreach and pressure people to conform. At the same time, individual rights are not absolute in every situation; public safety, disease control, and basic rules that let people live together do matter. The key is that society should have to justify any limit on personal rights very carefully, rather than assuming the group always wins. If we get that balance wrong, “the common good” can become a cover for trampling minorities and unpopular voices.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis berada di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai terlalu longgar, dan ujungnya justru bisa menekan minoritas, orang miskin, atau mereka yang suaranya tidak populer. Hak individu memang tidak mutlak dalam semua keadaan, tetapi pembatasannya harus sangat jelas, proporsional, dan benar-benar bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan sekadar karena mayoritas merasa benar. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling kuat menekan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi kebebasan dasar setiap orang sambil tetap menjaga ketertiban bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From a U.S. perspective, history shows how easily “the common good” gets used to justify censorship, discrimination, or government abuse, especially against unpopular minorities. That said, I do think some limits on individual rights are legitimate when there is a real, concrete harm to others, like public safety or disease control. The burden should always be on society to prove that a restriction is necessary and narrowly tailored, not on the individual to prove why their rights matter.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu: hak individu tidak boleh dikalahkan hanya karena sesuatu disebut kepentingan masyarakat. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, label “demi umum” terlalu sering dipakai longgar untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang akhirnya paling berat menimpa kelompok kecil, orang miskin, atau yang tidak punya suara. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dibuktikan nyata, proporsional, dan tidak boleh merusak martabat serta kebebasan dasar orang. Kalau tidak, “masyarakat” hanya jadi alasan untuk menekan individu, bukan melindunginya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the idea that society should generally outrank the rights of the individual, because in the United States that framing has too often been a license for abuse. The phrase "for the common good" sounds noble, but in practice it can be used to silence minorities, excuse censorship, or pressure people who do not fit the majority view. At the same time, I do not treat individual rights as unlimited; when there is a real and specific harm to others, society can justify narrow restrictions. The standard should be strict proof, not a vague appeal to the group.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
